description: Claude Code Channel MCP Server that bridges IRC messaging with Claude Code via MCP stdio protocol. The server connects to an IRC server as an agent nick, subscribes to channels, listens for @mentions and private messages, and forwards them to Claude Code as MCP notifications. It exposes two MCP tools (reply and join_channel) and handles system messages (stop, join, status) via the zchat-protocol. Additionally, it ships four slash-command definitions (reply, dm, join, broadcast) as Claude Code plugin commands, and an instructions.md template that configures Claude Code's message routing behavior including soul.md-based personality and subagent delegation.

key_symbols.name: inject_message
key_symbols.description: Send a channel notification to Claude Code via the MCP write stream. Converts IRC msg dict into a JSONRPCNotification with method 'notifications/claude/channel'.

key_symbols.name: poll_irc_queue
key_symbols.description: Async loop consuming IRC messages from asyncio.Queue and injecting them into Claude Code via inject_message.

key_symbols.name: setup_irc
key_symbols.description: Initialize IRC client (irc.client.Reactor), connect to IRC server, register event handlers (welcome/pubmsg/privmsg/disconnect), start reactor in daemon thread. Returns (connection, joined_channels).

key_symbols.name: on_welcome
key_symbols.description: IRC welcome handler — auto-joins channels from IRC_CHANNELS env var. Nested inside setup_irc.

key_symbols.name: on_pubmsg
key_symbols.description: IRC channel message handler — filters for @mentions of AGENT_NAME, cleans mention, enqueues to asyncio.Queue. Nested inside setup_irc.

key_symbols.name: on_privmsg
key_symbols.description: IRC private message handler — decodes sys messages via zchat_protocol; regular messages enqueued to asyncio.Queue. Nested inside setup_irc.

key_symbols.name: on_disconnect
key_symbols.description: IRC disconnect handler — sleeps 5s then attempts reconnect. Nested inside setup_irc.

key_symbols.name: _handle_sys_message
key_symbols.description: Dispatches incoming sys messages: sys.stop_request -> stop_confirmed, sys.join_request -> join channel + join_confirmed, sys.status_request -> status_response with channels/counters.

key_symbols.name: load_instructions
key_symbols.description: Loads instructions.md from same directory and interpolates $agent_name via string.Template.safe_substitute.

key_symbols.name: create_server
key_symbols.description: Creates mcp.server.lowlevel.Server instance named 'zchat-channel' with interpolated instructions.

key_symbols.name: register_tools
key_symbols.description: Registers MCP tools (reply, join_channel) on the Server instance. IRC connection resolved lazily from state dict.

key_symbols.name: _handle_reply
key_symbols.description: Implements 'reply' MCP tool — chunks text via chunk_message() then sends via connection.privmsg to chat_id.

key_symbols.name: _handle_join_channel
key_symbols.description: Implements 'join_channel' MCP tool — joins IRC channel by name.

key_symbols.name: main
key_symbols.description: Async entrypoint: creates queue, server, registers tools, opens MCP stdio streams, sets up IRC, runs server + poll_irc_queue concurrently via anyio.create_task_group.

key_symbols.name: entry_point
key_symbols.description: Synchronous entry point for console_scripts — calls asyncio.run(main()).

key_symbols.name: AGENT_NAME
key_symbols.description: Module-level constant from env var AGENT_NAME, default 'agent0'.

key_symbols.name: IRC_SERVER
key_symbols.description: Module-level constant from env var IRC_SERVER, default '127.0.0.1'.

key_symbols.name: IRC_PORT
key_symbols.description: Module-level constant from env var IRC_PORT, default 6667.

key_symbols.name: IRC_CHANNELS
key_symbols.description: Module-level constant from env var IRC_CHANNELS, default 'general'.

key_symbols.name: IRC_TLS
key_symbols.description: Module-level constant from env var IRC_TLS, default false.

key_symbols.name: _msg_counter
key_symbols.description: Module-level dict tracking sent/received message counts.

key_symbols.name: detect_mention
key_symbols.description: Check if message body contains @agent_name mention.

key_symbols.name: clean_mention
key_symbols.description: Remove @agent_name from message body and strip whitespace.

key_symbols.name: _sanitize_for_irc
key_symbols.description: Replace newlines with spaces for IRC single-line protocol.

key_symbols.name: chunk_message
key_symbols.description: Split message into chunks fitting within IRC 512-byte limit (default 390 bytes payload). Uses UTF-8 byte length, tries to break at spaces.

key_symbols.name: MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES
key_symbols.description: Constant 390 — max payload bytes per IRC message after reserving ~120 bytes for IRC header.

key_symbols.name: broadcast
key_symbols.description: Slash command definition for /zchat:broadcast. Sends a message to all joined IRC channels. Parses --text/-t and optional --channels/-C flags. Uses mcp__zchat-channel__reply tool.

key_symbols.name: dm
key_symbols.description: Slash command definition for /zchat:dm. Sends a private message to an IRC user. Parses --user/-u and --text/-t flags. Uses mcp__zchat-channel__reply tool.

key_symbols.name: join
key_symbols.description: Slash command definition for /zchat:join. Joins an IRC channel. Parses --channel/-c flag. Uses mcp__zchat-channel__join_channel tool.

key_symbols.name: reply
key_symbols.description: Slash command definition for /zchat:reply. Replies to an IRC channel or user. Parses --channel/-c and --text/-t flags. Uses mcp__zchat-channel__reply tool.

key_symbols.name: instructions_template
key_symbols.description: Agent instructions template with $agent_name placeholder. Defines message format (<channel> XML notifications), owner detection from nick prefix, idle/busy message handling strategy, subagent delegation, soul.md integration, system message handling, message priority table, and available slash commands.

key_symbols.name: project
key_symbols.description: Package zchat-channel-server v1.0.0.dev0, requires Python >=3.11. Console script entry: zchat-channel = server:entry_point.

key_symbols.name: pytest_config
key_symbols.description: Pytest config: testpaths=tests, markers e2e and prerelease, asyncio_mode=auto.

key_symbols.name: zchat-channel
key_symbols.description: MCP server definition: runs 'uv run --project ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} zchat-channel'.

key_symbols.name: plugin_manifest
key_symbols.description: Claude Code plugin manifest: name=zchat, description=IRC channel for Claude Code, version=0.1.0, keywords=[irc, channel, zchat].

key_symbols.name: test_sys_message_irc_roundtrip
key_symbols.description: Tests sys message encode/decode roundtrip via zchat_protocol: make -> encode -> decode preserves type and body.

key_symbols.name: test_sys_message_not_user_text
key_symbols.description: Tests that regular text and json-like strings are not decoded as sys messages.

key_symbols.name: test_load_instructions_interpolates_agent_name
key_symbols.description: Tests load_instructions() replaces $agent_name with actual agent name.

key_symbols.name: test_load_instructions_contains_routing_rules
key_symbols.description: Tests load_instructions() output contains all four slash commands, chat_id, and subagent/Agent tool references.

key_symbols.name: test_load_instructions_contains_soul_pointer
key_symbols.description: Tests load_instructions() output contains soul.md reference.

interfaces.name: inject_message(write_stream, msg: dict, context: str) -> None
setup_irc(queue: asyncio.Queue, loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop) -> tuple[connection, set[str]]
_handle_sys_message(msg: dict, sender_nick: str, connection, joined_channels: set) -> None
MCP Tool: reply(chat_id: str, text: str) -> TextContent

user_flows.name: MCP Server Startup
steps: entry_point() calls asyncio.run(main())
main() creates asyncio.Queue and MCP Server via create_server()
register_tools() registers reply + join_channel tools on server
MCP stdio_server opened (read_stream, write_stream)
2-second sleep for MCP handshake (server.py:305)
setup_irc() initializes IRC reactor, connects to IRC_SERVER:IRC_PORT as AGENT_NAME
IRC reactor runs in daemon thread (server.py:177)
anyio.create_task_group runs MCP server.run + poll_irc_queue concurrently

user_flows.name: Channel @mention -> Claude Code Notification
steps: IRC pubmsg event fires on_pubmsg (server.py:112)
detect_mention() checks for @AGENT_NAME in body (message.py:12)
clean_mention() strips @mention from body (message.py:17)
Message dict enqueued to asyncio.Queue via loop.call_soon_threadsafe (server.py:130)
poll_irc_queue() dequeues message (server.py:63)
inject_message() creates JSONRPCNotification with method 'notifications/claude/channel' (server.py:43)
Notification sent to Claude Code via write_stream (server.py:60)

user_flows.name: Private Message -> Claude Code Notification
steps: IRC privmsg event fires on_privmsg (server.py:133)
decode_sys_from_irc() checks if message is a sys message (server.py:140)
If not sys message, create message dict and enqueue to asyncio.Queue (server.py:153)
poll_irc_queue() -> inject_message() -> Claude Code notification (same as channel flow)

user_flows.name: System Message Handling (stop/join/status)
steps: IRC privmsg decoded as sys message by decode_sys_from_irc (server.py:140-141)
_handle_sys_message() dispatches by msg type (server.py:186)
sys.stop_request -> replies sys.stop_confirmed (server.py:189-191)
sys.join_request -> joins channel + replies sys.join_confirmed (server.py:192-199)
sys.status_request -> replies sys.status_response with channels/counters (server.py:200-206)

user_flows.name: MCP Tool: reply (send IRC message)
steps: Claude Code calls MCP tool 'reply' with chat_id + text
handle_call_tool dispatches to _handle_reply (server.py:264-265)
chunk_message() splits text into IRC-safe chunks (message.py:27)
Each chunk sent via connection.privmsg(chat_id, chunk) (server.py:276)
_msg_counter['sent'] incremented (server.py:277)

user_flows.name: MCP Tool: join_channel
steps: Claude Code calls MCP tool 'join_channel' with channel_name
handle_call_tool dispatches to _handle_join_channel (server.py:266-267)
connection.join(f'#{channel}') joins the IRC channel (server.py:282)

user_flows.name: Slash Command: /zchat:broadcast
steps: User invokes /zchat:broadcast -t 'message' [--channels '#ch1,#ch2']
Claude Code parses args per commands/broadcast.md
Determines joined channels from context or IRC_CHANNELS env
Calls MCP tool reply for each channel with the text

user_flows.name: Slash Command: /zchat:dm
steps: User invokes /zchat:dm -u alice -t 'hey'
Claude Code parses --user and --text per commands/dm.md
Calls MCP tool reply with chat_id=user nick, text=message
